{{< pagebreak >}}

# Your next 30 days {#next-30-days}

You do not need another program — only a calm, realistic pace for carrying one
small habit forward. Habits form over time, at a pace that varies from person to
person,^11^ and a simple when-then plan helps you keep going.^9^

```{=typst}
#wcg-group("Weeks 1–2 · settle one habit into ordinary days")
#wcg-field("The one habit I will keep practicing", lines: 2)

#wcg-group("Weeks 3–4 · keep what works; add only if it feels easy")
#wcg-field("What I will keep the same, or gently add", lines: 1)
#wcg-field("How I will handle a hard day", lines: 1)

#v(4pt)
#line(length: 100%, stroke: 0.6pt + c-taupe)
#v(4pt)

#wcg-group("Personal notes")
#wcg-field("One thing I want to remember", lines: 1)
#wcg-field("One small win from this week", lines: 1)
#wcg-notes(label: [Anything else worth keeping close], count: 5)

#v(1fr)
#line(length: 100%, stroke: 0.6pt + c-taupe)
#v(6pt)
#align(center)[
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 9.5pt, weight: 600, fill: c-num)[Keep going at ]#link("https://microsavingdaily.com")[#wcg-domain(size: 9.5pt)]
]
```
